articles.title: US Secretary Rubio: Iran expected to respond regarding negotiations today
articles.summary: US Secretary Rubio: Iran expected to respond regarding negotiations today US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told journalists in Rome that the US expecting a response from Iran today. "We’ll see what the response entails. ⁠The hope ⁠is it’s something that can put us ⁠into a serious ⁠process of negotiation." He described US-Iran clashes in the Strait of Hormuz as the US acting "defensively" to Iranian attacks. Rubio also stated that the US will not "negotiate with Iran over Hezbollah." "Lebanon should be governed by the Lebanese government," the Secretary added.

articles.title: Bahrain expels three MPs after they voted against royal decree on citizenship oversight
articles.summary: Bahrain expels three MPs after they voted against royal decree on citizenship oversight Bahrain ’s parliament has expelled three lawmakers after they voted against a royal decree removing judicial oversight in matters of citizenship, just days after dozens had their nationalities stripped in connection with the Iran war.  On Thursday morning, the Council of Representatives voted unanimously to revoke the memberships of Abdulnabi Salman, Mamdooh al-Saleh and Mahdi al-Shuwaik.  They were singled out due to their dissenting votes in a parliamentary session held on 28 April. Raed Mauqadi said settlers began groundwork for the outpost south of bypass Road 60, around 100 metres from schools and about 50 metres from nearby homes, according to the report. Israeli strikes on southern Lebanese town kills two
articles.summary: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanese town kills two Israeli strikes on the southern Lebanese town of Toura killed two people and wounded five others on Thursday, Lebanon's National News Agency reported. The wounded were taken to hospitals in the coastal city of Tyre, the report said. Israeli warplanes later carried out a second strike on Toura, and a separate Israeli raid also hit a house in the town of Tayr Falsayh, in the Tyre district, the agency said.
